title: Sh. Ram Rishi Gupta vs Sh. Hari Om on 30 August, 2014
body: Sh. Ram Rishi Gupta vs Sh. Hari Om on 30 August, 2014 IN THE COURT OF SH. DEVENDER KUMAR, ADJ-04, NEW DELHI DISTRICT, PATIALA HOUSE COURTS, NEW DELHI. MCA No. 5/14 Sh. Ram Rishi Gupta Through LRs R/o RZE-48-B, Gali no. 2, Main Sagar Pur, New Delhi - 110046 .... Appellant Vs. 1. Sh. Hari Om S/o Late Sh. Dharam Chand R/o WZ-1391/4, IInd Floor, Nangal Raya, New Delhi - 110046 2. Sh. Ashok Kumar S/o Late Sh. Dharam Chand R/o RZ-36, West Sagar Pur, New Delhi - 110046 3. Sh. Surender Kumar S/o Late Sh. Dharam Chand R/o WZ-1391/4, IInd Floor, Nangal Raya, New Delhi - 110046 .....Respondents Date of institution : 19.08.2014 Date on which reserved for judgment : 30.08.2014 Date of decision : 30.08.2014 Appeal under Section 99A of CPC MCA No.5/14 Sh. Ram Rishi Gupta Vs.Sh. Hari Om & Ors. 1/6 Judgment : Vide this judgement, I shall dispose of an appeal under Section 99A CPC filed by the appellant against the dismissal of objections under Section 47 alongwith application under Order 21 rule 58 and u/s. 31 of CPC alongwith another application under Section 340 Cr.PC vide order dated 11.08.2014 and also against issuance of warrant of possession to attach the property of the appellant. 1. Arguments on appeal heard. It is submitted by the counsel for the appellant that Ld. Trial Court has dismissed the application of the appellant under Order 21 rule 58 CPC alongwith another application under Section 31 CPC and objections under Section 47 CPC alongwith application under Section 340 Cr.PC by a combined order which is illegal. It is further argued on behalf of appellant that Ld. Trial Court while passing the order on the said applications failed to understand that the order to execute the decree under police protection had become unexecutable on 28.08.2008 when the lease of suit property already renewed by DH No. 1 after accepting an amount of Rs. 2,70,000/- against a receipt executed in the presence of two witnesses. It is further argued that the DH No. 1 to receive said amount of JD had also issued two undated cheques of Rs. 1,35,000/- (each) from salary account to the JD, for the purpose of encashment of the same, in case of renewal of tenancy was not accepted by respondent no. 2 & 3, but instead of relying upon the contention of the appellant, Ld. Trial Court has passed an order of issuance of warrant of possession against the tenanted premises in-haste. It is further submitted that even Ld. Trial Court refused to look into the original cheques produced by one of the LRs of the JD. It is further submitted that this court has jurisdiction to entertain this appeal as Ld. Trial Court has disposed off a number of applications by a single order and some of the orders are appealable. It is further argued that after making the payment by appellant, MCA No.5/14 Sh. Ram Rishi Gupta Vs.Sh. Hari Om & Ors. 2/6 DH was liable either to refund the amount or to renew the tenancy, but it was not done by the DH. It is further submitted that Ld. Trial Court was bound to decide the objections of the JD in a fair manner and also to ascertain the identification of the boundaries of the suit property to determine the number of suit property in terms of Section 31 of CPC, but instead of following the procedure, Ld. Civil Judge has issued the warrant of attachment against JD and dismissed all the applications. It is further argued that Ld. Civil Judge has failed to follow the procedure under Section 340 Cr.PC at the time of disposal of application of the appellant and whatever delay has been caused in the suit or execution cannot be solely attributed to the appellant. It is further submitted that this appeal is liable to be allowed and order of Ld. Trial Court is liable to be set aside. 2. On the other hand, Ld. Counsel for respondent has argued this appeal without filing any reply. It is argued on behalf of respondent that this appeal is not maintainable against dismissal of objections under Section 47 CPC and only remedy lies to file a revision petition. It is further submitted that the order passed by Ld. Trial Court is not bearing any illegality and infirmity and whatever pleaded by the appellant in this appeal is to be considered only in subsequent trial to recover the alleged amount of subsequent tenancy and it has nothing to do with the present case. It is further submitted that the case of the appellant is not covered within the parameter of order 21 rule 58 CPC as same is dealing with other types of properties which are likely to be attached by the court during execution and not a tenanted premises. It is further submitted that even complaint under section 340 Cr.PC was a prerogative of the court and mere dismissal of said application does not give any right to the appellant to file any such appeal. It is further submitted that this appeal is liable to be dismissed. MCA No.5/14 Sh. Ram Rishi Gupta Vs.Sh. Hari Om & Ors. 3/6 3. I have heard the arguments of both parties and perused the trial court record carefully. Before deciding this appeal on merit, it is to be seen as to whether the appeal is maintainable before this court or not. The order of Ld. Trial Court is inclusive of orders on applications under Section 31 , 47 CPC and another under order 21 rule 58 CPC and application under section 340 Cr.PC. Present appeal has been filed under Section 99A of CPC, however this provision is dealing with an altogether different type of appeals. As per section 99-A CPC, no order under section 47 shall be reversed or substantially varied, on account of any error, defect or irregularity in any proceeding relating to such order, unless such error, defect or irregularity has prejudicially affected the decision of the case. As such, the appeal of the appellant is not maintainable under this provision. The Hon'ble High Court of Delhi has dealt with the similar situation in (2009) 156 DLT 513 titled Yashpal Singh V. Bhagwana and has held that appeal under section 47 CPC is not maintainable and the Hon'ble High of Delhi has relied that the full bench decision of the Hon'ble Patna High Court pronounced in AIR 1987 Pat 33. In view of the legal ground alone this appeal is not maintainable. Further, admittedly this appeal has been filed against miscellaneous orders passed under the proceedings of execution petition and such type of appeals will be governed by order 43 rule 1 CPC and this appeal is not falling under the said provisions. 4. So far the objections of the appellant under Order 21 rule 58 CPC are concerned, Court is bound to entertain any claim or objection against the attachment of any property on the ground that such property is not liable to such attachment, but such objections cannot be entertained where the court considers that the claim or objection was designedly or unnecessarily delayed. Ld. Trial Court has dealt with the application of the appellant and decided it in detail. MCA No.5/14 Sh. Ram Rishi Gupta Vs.Sh. Hari Om & Ors. 4/6 It was the main argument of the appellant before the Ld. Trial Court under order 21 rule 58 CPC that the DH entered into a fresh lease deed with the JD and accepted the amount of Rs. 2,70,000/- on 28.08.2008 and similar objection has been taken up before this court as well. However, the appellant has failed to explain either before Ld. Trial Court or this court as to what action was initiated by the appellant against the respondent after renewal of tenancy. If a decree had already been passed against the JD then appellant was duty bound to get the matter settled only through the court concerned, otherwise appellant was well aware about the consequences. During the course of arguments, Ld. Counsel for appellant has argued before this court that the amount paid by the JD should be secured, but arguments of Ld. Counsel for appellant have no force as the main motive of the JD seems to get the said amount allegedly paid to the DH on the pretext of renewal of tenancy. If the DH has accepted the amount and failed to execute a fresh lease deed as alleged by the JD then JD has ever option to recover the said amount before appropriate court of law but that amount cannot be directed to be repaid in this proceedings. Even the appellant allegedly paid the amount to the JD outside the court and without proving this fact before this court JD is not liable in this proceedings. The appellant has annexed the photocopies of receipt and cheque on record alongwith objections under Order 21 rule 58 CPC . As per objections, main plea taken by the appellant is with regard to the payment and renewal of tenancy which is also part of objections under Section 47 CPC and similar plea is not maintainable in this appeal. The remaining objections under Order 21 rule 58 CPC are mainly with regard to payment of rent by the JD to DH and on this ground alone the appellant has stated that the decree is not unexecutable or drawn accordingly. All these objections are not maintainable. MCA No.5/14 Sh. Ram Rishi Gupta Vs.Sh. Hari Om & Ors. 5/6 5. So far the plea of the respondent with regard of rejection of application under Section 340 Cr.PC is concerned, it was the prerogative of the court as to whether to make a criminal complaint under Section 340 Cr.PC or not. If court considered that making a complaint under Section 340 Cr.PC was not expedient and in the interest of justice, then court was not bound to make a complaint. As such, Section 340 Cr. PC does not provide any mandate to the court to make a complaint at the instance of any party. As such, this application is also not maintainable 6. Keeping in view the fact and circumstances of the case, I found no illegality or infirmity in the order of Ld. Trial Court and this appeal is not maintainable, hence dismissed. Interim order dated 11.08.2014 is hereby vacated. Ld. Trial Court is directed to proceed the matter further as per law. Copy of order be sent to the concerned court alongwith TCR. Appeal file be consigned to record room after due compliance. Dated:- 30.08.2014 (Devender Kumar) Announced in the open Court. ADJ-04, New Delhi District, Patiala House Courts, Delhi. MCA No.5/14 Sh. Ram Rishi Gupta Vs.Sh. Hari Om & Ors. 6/6
